description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a daisy-like flower with a prominent yellow central disc and numerous white petals radiating outward. The petals are slightly irregular, with some edges appearing jagged or torn, giving a natural, organic look. The central disc is textured and dome-shaped, typical of composite flowers in the Asteraceae family. The flower is attached to a slender, green stem that appears slightly curved and tapers toward the base. No leaves are visible along the stem, and the flower head is the most visually dominant feature. The overall appearance suggests a wildflower commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or roadside verges.
description: A white-petaled daisy-like flower with a yellow central disc and a slender green stem, featuring slightly jagged petal edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flower, with the yellow disc and white petals clearly visible and slightly irregular edges.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the dome shape of the yellow disc and the layering of the white petals.
description_view_2: Profile view emphasizing the thinness of the flower and the curvature of the green stem beneath the flower head.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the underside of the petals and the attachment point of the stem, with some petal overlap visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial pattern around the central disc, with each petal emerging from the same point at the base. The arrangement is actinomorphic, displaying radial symmetry, and the petals slightly overlap in places, creating a layered effect typical of daisies.
